title: Nobel Conference 2025 - Frank Hu
text:
I'm going to start by just saying I love
sugar. Yes, I said it. I love it. Uh my
kids know that after Halloween it's in
their best interest to hide their candy
from me. Sweet treats are one small
example of the way food can be
associated with fun, celebration, joy,
and comfort. Our first speaker this
morning once said, "Food is love. Food
is joy. Food is culture." But like every
great heroic fantasy film, the light
always is balanced by some form of dark.
While sugar can inspire joy, it can also
bring addiction. Sugar offers the body
an easily accessible form of fuel for
physical activity, but it can also
contribute to chronic disease, disease
risks such as type 2 diabetes and
obesity. The human body is beautifully
complicated and Doctor Who's research
aims to help the world understand a bit
more about why each of our bodies
recognizes and utilizes something like
sugar so differently. Doctor Who is
trying to uncover the mysteries of human
metabolism and offer insights into how
sugar specifically is connected to
health outcomes. What is the role of
genetics or lifestyle? It's complicated
and it will always be complicated.
Doctor Who may not tell us exactly how
much sugar is healthy, nor will he be
able to promise that if guidelines are
adopted that health will change. His
research helps us peel back one layer of
the conversation related to sugar
consumption and health. Dr. Who is the
Frederick J. Stair Professor of
Nutrition and Epidemiology at the
Harvard Thchan School of Public Health
and Professor of Medicine at Bringham
and Women's Hospital at Harvard Medical
School. His work has focused on factors
related to sugar metabolism and chronic
disease risk associated with sugar
intake in the diet. Please help me give
a warm Gustavis welcome to Doctor Who.
[Music]
Thank you.
Good morning.

I'm very excited about the programs in

the largest candy store in Minnesota.
uh uh that's really a perfect

uh all over the world even in low and

And this is the obesity map in the US.

So the question is why not Minnesota?
Why Colorado? We don't know. Um and this

consumption of sugary beverage map for

in the US. Can you believe that almost
60%
US children aged 1 to five years

We are living in the obesic food
environment
dominated by ultrarocessed foods or

market especially to children. Uh they

When we talk about sugar, we have to
distinguish different type of sugars. We
have monac monossaccharides like
glucose, fructose or disaccharides like

fructose corn syrups is a mixture of

Even though high fructose corn syrup has

it's metabolically not very different
from table sugar or or canned sugar. We
have natural sugars in fruits and
vegetables and dairy. And we also have a
lot of added sugars uh in um packaged or
processed foods.
There's no question that American diet
is too sweet. According to the National
Health Nutrition Survey, Americans
consume on average 17 teaspoons of added
sugar per day. 17 teaspoons. That's
equivalent to 270 calories. Most of
those added sugars come from
beverages, especially uh sugar
sweetening beverages or SSBs.
One can of soda contains 10 teaspoons of
sugar,
almost 140 calories. In the US,
sugar beverages and other foods are
sweetened by high fructose corn syrups
because it's much cheaper and it's more

sugar.
Besides sodas and fruit drinks, um we

who do not play sports.
In the past several decades, the portion
size of sodas have been superersized.
In the movie theater, you can get 64 oz
of soda. Do you know how many calories
in 64 oz of soda?
Almost 800 calories. And how much time
do you need to run to burn all those

two hours. It's at least one and a half
hours for most people. It's like running

calories.
Added sugars have been implicated in
wide range of health conditions
including dental carries, weight gain,
obesity, diabetes, metabolic syndrome,
cardiovascular disease, gout,
non-alcoholic fatty liver disease and
even some cancers.
Most of the evidence has come from
epidemiological studies. This is a
hierarchy of study designs in nutrition
epidemiology. The highest level of
evidence of course come from randomized
clinical trials with heart clinical
outcomes like a heart attack or stroke
or diabetes but large long-term RCTs are
typically not feasible because of the
high cost low compliance and ethical
concerns. So this next level of evidence
come from small randomized clinical
trials with intermediate outcomes like
body weight or blood sugar or not
carefully controlled prospective cohort
studies. So when you have evidence from
observational cohorts and uh small
intervention trials with intermediate
outcomes, we have to rely on certain
criteria to make causal inference. So
the Bradford hail criteria have been
used in epidemiological studies to make
causal inference and those criteria were
used in for example uh in drawing the
causal relationship between smoking and
lung cancer and the intake of trans fat
and the coronary heart disease. Of
course, we also have evidence from other
type of studies like case control
studies, ecological studies, migration
studies and cross-sectional studies. But
the rig of the evidence from those type
of studies is much less compared to
randomized clinical trials or large
prospective cohort studies. So today the
evidence I'm going to present mostly
come from those two type of studies
because of the advances in omix
technologies and big data analytics and
AI there has been a paradigm shift in
nutrition research from the so-called
blackbox epidemiology to systems
epidemiology.
This approach enable us to incorporate
system biology tools like omics into
large epidemiological studies and the
intervention trials. It allow us to
enuminate biological mechanisms,
identify novel exposure and disease
biomarkers, improve disease predictions,
identify high-risk individuals and
populations, and also identify new
intervention targets.
the nurses health studies conducted at
Harvard uh one of the largest longest
running cohort studies uh globally. Uh
the initial nurses health study was
established in 1976 almost 50 years ago.
So next year we will celebrate a
milestone which is the 50 year
anniversary of the original nurses
health study. Subsequently, nurses

study three cohorts were established and
the guts cohort include participants who
are the children of nurses health study
2. So we have multi-generational data

or among mothers on their offspring
health.
Over the years we have collected
extensive data using questions. We have
collected hundreds thousands of
biopecimen samples including urine,

environmental uh data and we have
dipmented hundreds of uh health outcomes
and the disease outcomes.
Every two to four years we have been

forth. Diet assessment is one of the
most challenging aspects of uh cohort

including uh validated self reported

Besides traditional dietary biomarkers,
we have conducted extensive omics
analysis including genomics,
metabolomics,
metagenomics and epigenomics. So
incorporating the OMIX data into our
large cohort studies allow us to better
understand the biological mechanisms
underline the relationship between diet
and disease outcomes.
This is a meta analysis we published
recently. This meta analysis include 40
cohort studies uh conducted among
children. Uh 21 uh cohorts conducted
among adults. The results are very
consistent. Uh what we found is that
there is a consistent dose response
relationship between increased added
sugar intake and the increased waking.
In this meta analysis we also include
eight RCTs conducted among children 16
RCTs conducted adults. Again there is a
strong consistent evidence that SSBs
promote weekend in both children and
adults.
Uh this is the largest intervention
trial conduct in children and in this
study more than 640
children were randomized to two groups
SSB group or sugar-free beverage group.
The study design was very clever. Uh the
because the soda cans for the two groups
look identical. So the most of the
children couldn't tell which group they
were in. So this is uh truly double
blinded uh intervention trial. The
intervention lasted for one and a half
years and you can see that at the end of

group, the sugar-free group had
significantly
reduced incidence of childhood
overweight and obesity. So this study
provide very strong evidence that
reducing SSB consumption could have
significant benefits in reducing
childhood obesity.
In this meta analysis, we summarize the
findings from um several large cohort
studies. We found that overall one to
two serving per day of SSBs is
associated with 26% increased risk of
developing type 2 diabetes and this
positive association remains significant
even after adjustment for BMI and the
total uh energy intake.
SSB consumption is also consistent
associated with increased risk of
hypertension, coronary heart disease,
stroke and this is a very large study uh
using uh data from enhance which is the
national representative sample of the US
adults and we found a strong dose
response relationship between added
sugar intake and the risk of
cardiovascular mortality. As you can see
that the increased risk start to take
off around 10% of calories from added
sugar and the increased risk accelerated
when the intake uh is above uh 20% of
calories from added sugar. So compared
to individuals who consume less than 10%
calories from added sugar, those who

sugar had 30% increased cardiovascular

of added sugar, almost one quarter of
their calories from added sugar, their
risk of cardiovascular mortality
was increased by almost three-fold. even
after taking into account wide range of

say there may be only a few people who

actually not true. If you look at

SSB consumption has also been as
consistently associated with increased

But added sugar is only small piece of

Um so that's why in the recent uh study

participants
met those five criteria only 10%. And
what we want to do is to see what kind

What we found is that all healthy

Uh the strongest benefit was observed

healthy aging. So you can see that sugar

Other foods including uh added sugar or

So the question is how much is too much?
The dietary guidelines for Americans and
who recommend less than 10% of daily

And there is strong evidence that liquid

because they contain zero calories. So

year marketing
